findings.message: New `requests` dependency is unpinned — no version specifier means resolution drifts across installs and any future breaking release lands unreviewed. Every other dependency in this manifest uses a `>=X.Y` floor; this one should too.
findings.suggestion: Add a lower bound, e.g. `"requests>=2.32"`, and let the lockfile / `pip-audit` (already a dev dep) own the upper bound.

findings.message: `requests` duplicates capability already covered by existing dependencies. The project ships FastAPI + an async stack, and `httpx` is already in the dev group (used by the test client); `httpx` is a drop-in for `requests` with sync AND async support, HTTP/2, and active maintenance, whereas `requests` is in maintenance-only mode upstream. Adding a second HTTP client increases supply-chain surface (urllib3, charset-normalizer, idna, certifi) for no new capability.
findings.suggestion: Drop `requests` and use `httpx` (promote it from dev to runtime if a runtime HTTP client is actually needed). If `requests` is genuinely required by a third-party SDK, justify it in a comment on the dependency line.

findings.message: Dependency addition lacks a stated justification. Manifest changes that add a runtime HTTP client to a service that already has one are worth a one-line rationale (e.g. which call site needs it) so reviewers can confirm the need rather than infer it.
findings.suggestion: Either reference the introducing call site in the PR description, or remove the dep if it was added speculatively.
